q: The major types of renewable energy sources listed include solar, wind, hydropower, ocean, geothermal, and _.
Earth's atmosphere and clouds absorb or scatter as much as _ % of incoming sunlight.
Above the earth's atmosphere, solar radiation has an intensity of approximately _ $\text{W m}^{-2}$.
The solar radiation intensity value of approximately 1350 $\text{W m}^{-2}$ is known as the _.
Another commonly used term for the irradiance of sunlight is _.
At the earth's surface, solar radiation has an intensity of approximately _ $\text{W m}^{-2}$ on a clear day at solar noon in the summer.
According to the text, there are _ main ways to harness solar energy.
The conversion of light energy into electrical energy is based on a phenomenon called the _ effect.
A solar cell is structurally a basic _ junction diode.
Individual solar cells can be combined to form modules commonly known as _.
Achieving perfect _ sun rays provides the optimal orientation so solar panels receive direct light throughout the day.
Power conversion efficiency of a solar panel is the ratio of electrical power delivered by the panel to the solar power _ by it.
Solar water heating systems installed in homes are comprised of _ main elements.
The main elements of a solar water heating system include a solar collector, insulated piping, and a hot water _ tank.
_ collectors are the most common type of collector used in solar heating.
Flat plate collectors contain _ pipes attached to an absorber plate inside an insulated box.
Concentrated solar power (CSP) systems generate solar power by using mirrors or _ to focus a large area of sunlight.
The heat collected in a CSP system is primarily used to create _, which drives a turbine.
CSP plants structurally consist of _ main functional parts.
There are _ types of CSP technologies explicitly listed in the text.
Which of the following is an official CSP technology type mentioned in the text?
The band gap of GaAs mentioned in the reviewed exercise is _ eV.
In Example 4.2, the specific heat capacity of water $c$ is given as _ $\text{J kg}^{-1}\text{ K}^{-1}$.
According to Example 4.2, a value of $1 \text{ kWh}$ is mathematically equal to _ $\text{J}$.
A wind turbine converts wind energy directly into _.
With _ percent of the world's oil reserves already discovered, finding new ways to make energy is vital.
Energy demand is a term specifically used to describe the consumption of energy by _ activities.
Non-renewable energy resources take a long time, often _ of years, to be replenished.
Renewable resources naturally replenish over short periods, meaning they are in a virtually _ supply.
Fossil fuel liquids are refined into _ products used as ingredients in manufacturing items like plastics.
Wind energy is the energy generated or produced by harnessing the power of the ___.
Harnessing the wind is highly dependent on weather and ___.
Windmills convert wind energy directly into ___ energy.
A wind turbine converts wind energy into ___.
Wind farms consist of many individual wind turbines, which are connected to the electric power ___ network.
Wind energy is the ___ energy of air in motion.
In the formula for total wind energy, the variable $\rho$ represents the ___ of air.
The wind power incident on area $A$ is given by the formula $P = \frac{1}{2}\rho A v^3$, where $A$ is the ___.
a: rotor area of a wind turbine

q: The output of a wind turbine is proportional to the dimensions of the rotor and to the ___ of the wind speed.
Theoretically, when wind speed doubles, wind power potential increases by a factor of ___.
Wind turbines are mounted on a tower to take advantage of the faster and less ___ wind.
Usually, two or three blades are mounted on a shaft to form a ___.
Hydropower, or hydroelectric power, is one of the oldest and largest sources of ___ energy.
The source of power in a hydroelectric plant is the ___ of water.
There are three types of hydropower facilities: impoundment, diversion, and ___.
The most common type of hydroelectric power plant is an ___ facility.
In an impoundment facility, the difference in the two water elevations is known as the ___.
A closed conduit that channels the flow of water to turbines with water flow regulated by gates and valves is a ___.
In a hydroelectric plant, transformers are used to convert the alternating voltage of the generators to a ___ voltage.
A diversion facility channels a portion of a river through a canal or a penstock to utilize the natural decline of the river bed ___.
When the demand for electricity is low, a PSH facility stores energy by pumping water from the lower reservoir to an ___.
In the formula $P = \eta \rho \dot{V} g h$, the dimensionless variable $\eta$ represents the ___.
The volumetric flow rate $\dot{V}$ in the hydroelectric power formula is measured in ___.
A water turbine converts the kinetic energy of falling water into ___ energy.
Tidal energy is produced by the difference in ___.
Tidal energy is harnessed by constructing a dam, known as a tidal ___, across a narrow opening to the sea.
The waves near the seashore are generated by strong ___ blowing across the sea.
Ocean thermal energy conversion plants can operate if the temperature difference between the water at the surface and water at depths up to $2\text{ km}$ is ___ or more.
In ocean thermal energy conversion plants, the warm surface-water is used to boil a volatile liquid such as ___.
Geothermal energy is a type of renewable energy taken from the ___.
Extremely hot liquid and semi-liquid rock located under Earth's surface is called ___.
Biomass is renewable organic material that comes from ___.
Biomass contains stored ___ energy.
The most common method for converting biomass to useful energy is ___.
Thermochemical conversion of biomass includes pyrolysis and ___.
Biomass gasification produces a carbon monoxide and hydrogen rich gas called ___.
The chemical conversion process used for converting vegetable oils, animal fats, and greases into biodiesel is ___.
Biological conversion of biomass includes fermentation and ___ digestion.
Anaerobic digestion of biomass produces natural gas, which is also called ___.
